name: Anaplan

leadership.name: Charlie Gottdiener
leadership.title: Chief Executive Officer
leadership.background: Previously President and CEO of Neustar for four years. Earlier held strategy roles at Providence Equity, D&B, BCG, and Cap Gemini Ernst & Young. Architect of the 'decision excellence' strategy and $500M R&D roadmap.

leadership.name: Hemant Kapadia
leadership.title: Chief Financial Officer
leadership.background: 30+ years in finance. Prior CFO roles at Sirius Computer Solutions, Mark Andy Inc., and SunEdison Semiconductor. Senior finance roles at United Technologies and GE.

leadership.name: Greg Randolph
leadership.title: President and Chief Revenue Officer
leadership.background: 25+ years leading go-to-market at public and PE-owned software companies. Most recently Chief Commercial Officer at e2open (supply chain SaaS). Replaced prior CRO in September 2025.

leadership.name: Adam Thier
leadership.title: Chief Product and Technology Officer
leadership.background: 20+ years in enterprise software and FP&A. Previously held product and technology roles at SAP, Hyperion, Vianai, and Adaytum. Was a Thoma Bravo Operating Advisor before joining.

leadership.name: Hyune Hand
leadership.title: Chief Customer Officer
leadership.background: Previously President of Sales & Marketing at Park Place Technologies and SVP Customer Engagement at Neustar (TransUnion), where she drove retention into the mid-90% range.

leadership.name: Jim Freeze
leadership.title: Chief Marketing Officer
leadership.background: 25+ years in revenue-focused leadership across SaaS, AI, security, and telecom. Previously CMO at Interactions, Aspect Software, and Chief Commercial Officer at VETRO.

ipoReadiness: High — confidential S-1 filing underway as of January 2026 per The Information. Thoma Bravo ~3.5yr hold. Rule of 55 achieved.

businessOverview: Anaplan is a cloud-native connected planning platform for enterprise finance, sales, supply chain, and HR — the largest independent pure-play EPM vendor by ARR.
Founded 2006, taken private by Thoma Bravo in June 2022 at $10.7B EV (~14x ARR). ARR crossed $1B as of early 2025, up from ~$700M at go-private.
Platform is powered by the proprietary Hyperblock multidimensional calculation engine — deeply embedded in enterprise workflows with high switching costs.
Three BUs: Finance (>$600M ARR, core; 9x Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader), Sales & Marketing (>$200M ARR), Supply Chain (~$200M ARR, fastest-growing).
200+ customers with >$1M ACV, representing more than half of aggregate ARR. Customers include ~half of the Fortune 50.
Preparing confidential IPO filing as of January 2026 per The Information — likely 2026 public market re-entry if market conditions cooperate.
brief.productModel: Annual SaaS subscription (ARR-based). Land-and-expand motion: Finance BU is the primary land (CFO buyer), followed by cross-sell into Sales/Supply Chain. Recently added Anaplan Data Orchestrator (enterprise data integration) and acquired Fluence Technologies (financial close/consolidation) in April 2024 to extend the EPM suite. $500M multi-year R&D roadmap underway including role-based AI agents launched December 2025.

recentNews.headline: Thoma Bravo-Backed Anaplan Prepares Confidential IPO Filing
recentNews.whyItMatters: Anaplan filing confidentially signals Thoma Bravo is positioning for a public exit roughly four years post take-private, validating the operational transformation thesis. Valuation and timing will be a key read-through for the broader enterprise software IPO pipeline.

recentNews.headline: Anaplan Introduces Role-Based AI Agents for Enterprise Planning
recentNews.whyItMatters: Launch of agentic AI (Finance Analyst, Custom Agent) embeds AI into Anaplan's core platform, competing with Workday, SAP, and Oracle on the AI-native EPM narrative directly ahead of a likely IPO.

recentNews.headline: Anaplan Reveals Extended AI Portfolio Roadmap and Launches Four New Applications
recentNews.whyItMatters: Four simultaneous AI app launches anchored to a $500M R&D commitment is the most significant product expansion since the take-private, setting up the AI growth narrative for public market investors.

recentNews.headline: Anaplan Achieves $1B+ ARR and Rule of 55 Profitability in Business Transformation Milestone
recentNews.whyItMatters: First substantive financial disclosure since going private — $1B ARR, Rule of 55 profitability, 300 new logos, 200+ $1M+ ACV customers. Classic pre-IPO market conditioning playbook.

recentNews.headline: Anaplan Lays Off Hundreds Across U.S. and U.K.
recentNews.whyItMatters: Post-acquisition workforce reduction reflects the standard Thoma Bravo playbook of cost rationalization to build EBITDA and reach Rule of 40+ efficiency profile required for a re-listing.

recentNews.headline: Anaplan Acquires Fluence Technologies to Expand Financial Close Capabilities
recentNews.whyItMatters: Bolt-on into financial close and consolidation extends the EPM suite beyond planning into the full CFO workflow, directly competing with OneStream and Oracle EPM on completeness of offering.

keyMetrics.operatingMargin: Rule of 55 (positive EBITDA, transformed from negative at go-private)
keyMetrics.customers: 200+ with >$1M ACV; ~300 new logos added past year; ~half of Fortune 50

earningsTakeaways.aiCommentary: CEO Gottdiener positioning Anaplan around 'decision excellence' and AI. $500M R&D roadmap. 40% increase in software engineering headcount. McKinsey interview thesis: companies with tighter decision cycles outperform peers by ~14pp TSR. Supply Chain cited as fastest-growing BU.
earningsTakeaways.demandCommentary: No direct macro commentary (private). ~300 new logos and 200+ $1M ACV crossings imply healthy enterprise demand. Asian market expansion cited as incremental growth driver. IPO preparation signals sponsor confidence in demand trajectory.

keyRisks: Growth deceleration under Thoma Bravo: ARR CAGR of ~13-15% since go-private vs. 24%+ public-era growth — re-IPO story depends on showing re-acceleration
Competitive displacement by Pigment (fast-growing AI-native challenger) eating into Anaplan's mid-market and emerging enterprise accounts
OneStream (now Hg-backed) dominant in financial close/consolidation after Fluence acquisition — direct overlap with Anaplan's expanded suite
PE ownership debt load: Thoma Bravo typically leverages buyouts; high-interest debt servicing could constrain R&D investment relative to better-capitalized public peers
NRR unknown: public-era NRR was ~120%+ but post-go-private efficiency initiatives (headcount reductions, S&M cuts) may have pressured net retention in the near-term
Workday Adaptive Planning bundled into HCM suite creates price-compression risk in competitive deals where Workday is already the HCM vendor

comps.name: Workday
comps.note: Closest public HCM/ERP SaaS comp; Adaptive Planning is Anaplan's direct FP&A competitor. $32B market cap. Multiple compressed vs. prior years on decelerating growth.
comps.source: yfinance, close of 2026-06-03 (recomputed: last close × shares + net debt)

comps.name: SAP SE
comps.note: ERP incumbent; SAP Analytics Cloud competes directly with Anaplan. Premium multiple reflects $77B cloud backlog and AI narrative.
comps.source: yfinance, close of 2026-06-03 (recomputed: last close × shares + net debt)

comps.name: Salesforce
comps.note: Large-cap SaaS floor multiple. $153B market cap. Competes via Tableau and budgeting add-ons.
comps.source: yfinance, close of 2026-06-03 (recomputed: last close × shares + net debt)

comps.name: Veeva Systems
comps.note: Vertical SaaS premium comp. $3.2B revenue, $21.9B EV. High switching costs — upside scenario for Anaplan IPO multiple if planning platform narrative holds.
comps.source: yfinance, close of 2026-06-03 (recomputed: last close × shares + net debt)

comps.name: Oracle
comps.note: Multiple distorted by OCI cloud infra hypergrowth. $64B LTM revenue, ~$630B EV. Less relevant as pure EPM comp — treat as ceiling only.
comps.source: yfinance, close of 2026-06-03 (recomputed: last close × shares + net debt)

comps.name: OneStream (Hg)
comps.note: MOST RELEVANT COMP. Direct EPM pure-play, $602M ARR, $6.4B Hg take-private at ~10.6x revenue (Apr 2026). Same category, similar scale. Key anchor for Anaplan re-IPO valuation at $1B ARR.

comps.name: Pigment
comps.note: $1B valuation / ~$100M ARR at Series D (ICONIQ, Apr 2024). $397M total raised. Fast-growing AI-native challenger eating into Anaplan enterprise accounts.

comps.name: Vena Solutions
comps.note: ~$116M ARR (Sacra 2024). Excel-native FP&A. Vista Equity-backed ($476M raised). Mid-market focus.

comps.name: Planful
comps.note: ~$144M ARR (est.). Vector Capital-backed. 1,500+ customers. AI Predict suite launched 2024. Subscale vs. Anaplan; mid-market competitive reference.

comps.name: Mosaic (acq. by HiBob)
comps.note: $35M acquisition (Feb 2025). Signals the floor for subscale FP&A exits. NOT a relevant multiple comp for Anaplan — included for competitive landscape completeness only.

slideBullets: Anaplan crossed $1B ARR as of early 2025 under Thoma Bravo ownership — up from ~$700M at go-private in June 2022, implying ~13-15% CAGR with Rule of 55 profitability
Connected planning platform addresses the full enterprise planning stack: Finance (>$600M ARR), Sales & Marketing (>$200M ARR), and Supply Chain (~$200M ARR, fastest-growing BU)
Preparing confidential IPO filing as of January 2026 — Thoma Bravo signaling a public exit at ~3.5yr hold; OneStream's $6.4B Hg buyout at ~13x revenue is the key comp for valuation framing
AI agentic roadmap launched December 2025 (role-based Finance Analyst and Custom Agents) plus $500M R&D commitment positions Anaplan on the AI-native EPM narrative ahead of the re-listing
200+ customers with >$1M ACV; ~half of Fortune 50 on platform; Fluence Technologies acquisition (April 2024) extends suite into financial close, expanding the CFO wallet share opportunity
diligenceQuestions: What is the current NRR and how has it trended since the go-private? The PE efficiency playbook (S&M cuts, headcount reductions) often pressures net retention in the near-term — this is the single most important metric for the re-IPO story.

How is Anaplan structurally defending against Pigment at the enterprise level? Pigment is doubling ARR annually and winning 57% of its new revenue from enterprise — what is Anaplan's win rate in competitive bake-offs post-2023?

What is the debt load from the Thoma Bravo leveraged buyout and how does it compare to estimated FCF generation? Rule of 55 is a broad metric — understanding the FCF yield and debt service requirements is critical for valuation and IPO capital structure.
